title: Empatojayos Brand
content:

{{Quote|He is a Jedi…a man of honor!|Ganathan crewman|<ref name="DEII 4" />}}
'''Empatojayos Brand''' {{C|{{Audio|EmpatojayosBrand.flac|Pronunciation}}}} was a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and survivor of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]. A [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Yaddle/Legends|Yaddle]], Brand graduated to Knighthood after the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. He served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] in the following [[Standard year/Legends|years]], and in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] was [[Third mission to the Sepan sector|sent]] to negotiate an end to the [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]]-long [[Sepan Civil War]], albeit unsuccessfully. Within months the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] began, and Brand flew a [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7 ''Aethersprite''-class light interceptor]] as part of a [[Jedi assault team/Legends|Jedi strike team]] in the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]] that opened the war. Brand became a [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] during the war, leading his [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] to victory in the [[Battle of Bassadro]].

When the Clone Wars ended, however, the Jedi were [[Order 66/Legends|declared]] enemies of the Republic and Brand was hunted down by [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Brand's [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was damaged by Vader near the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], and the grievously injured Brand was left to [[Death/Legends|die]]. However, he was rescued by the nearby and technologically primitive [[Ganathan]] people. They fitted him with a [[Cybernetics/Legends|prosthetic body]], and Brand became the [[King/Legends|King]] of [[Ganath/Legends|Ganath]].

In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], a damaged ship called the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] arrived in [[Ganathan Space]]. Brand learned from its passengers, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], that Vader had been [[Battle of Endor/Legends|defeated]], but the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war]] continued to rage against [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who had discovered a way to cheat death by [[Transfer essence|transferring]] his consciousness between [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] bodies. Brand also learned that Leia's brother [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] was building a [[New Jedi Order]], and so he accompanied the [[Solo family/Legends|Solos]] back to their safeworld of [[New Alderaan/Legends|New Alderaan]]. After an [[Battle of New Alderaan (Operation Shadow Hand)|Imperial attack]], Brand joined Skywalker and the New Republic in their flight from the Empire. Brand eventually accompanied Skywalker in a last-ditch [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|attempt]] at stopping Palpatine on the world of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]. Though injured by Palpatine in the fight, Brand was able to capture his opponent's [[Soul/Legends|soul]], which fled its body after being shot by Han Solo, and upon dying Brand carried it to the [[Netherworld of the Force/Legends|Netherworld of the Force]], where the former Emperor would be held [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] by all departed Jedi for eternity.

==Biography==
===Life as a Jedi===
{{Quote|It has been many years since I have seen a Jedi Knight…|Empatojayos Brand|<ref name="DEII 4" />}}
[[File:Yaddle-SWE.png|thumb|left|200px|Brand's master, Yaddle]]
Empatojayos Brand was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Yaddle/Legends|Yaddle]]. Brand passed the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Jedi Trials]] and became a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] after the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]]<ref name="NEGTC" /> in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="NEC" /> He would be Yaddle's last apprentice.<ref name="NEGTC" /> Brand received some of his [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] from Master [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] as well,<ref name="Yoda">{{DB|character|yoda|Yoda}}</ref> including that of [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]].<ref name="DEII 6" /> In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="Sepan">[[HoloNet News Vol. 531 49|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #49]] provides a [[Great ReSynchronization]] date of 13:3:28. This date corresponds to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] on the [[Galactic Standard Calendar]], according to [[Template:YearMathGRS|the following explanation]].</ref> Brand was [[Third mission to the Sepan sector|dispatched]] along with fellow [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Bultar Swan/Legends|Bultar Swan]] and [[Chellemi Chuovvick]] to the [[Sepan sector/Legends|Sepan sector]] to negotiate a peace to the long-running [[Sepan Civil War]] between the worlds of [[Dimok/Legends|Dimok]] and [[Ripoblus/Legends|Ripoblus]]. During the [[Standard month/Legends|month]]-long negotiations, hostilities came to a halt, but upon the Jedi's return to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] fighting flared up again.<ref name="HNN1">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|jediwatch/13328_2.html|Sepan Civil War Escalates}}</ref>

Only months later,<ref name="Months">The [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]] occurred on the date [[Great ReSynchronization|13:5:21]], according to ''[[Republic HoloNet News Special Inaugural Edition 16:5:241]]''. That corresponds to roughly two [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the end of the mission to [[Sepan sector/Legends|Sepan sector]] on 13:3:28, detailed in ''[[HoloNet News Vol. 531 49]]''</ref> war broke out on the planet [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], as Jedi and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] new [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] were dispatched to that world to rescue the captured Jedi [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] from [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], leader of the secessionist [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Brand [[Pilot/Legends|flew]] a [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7 ''Aethersprite''-class light interceptor]] into battle with a [[Jedi assault team/Legends|strike team]] led by Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]]. Upon arriving at Geonosis, Brand joined with Master [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]]'s [[Zeta Squadron (Jedi Order)|Zeta Squadron]] to stop [[Cavik Toth]], an ally of Dooku, from deploying a chemical weapon called [[trihexalon]], or "hex," onto the surface of Geonosis. Brand and his wing intercepted Toth's [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] above Geonosis. Between Brand and the other Jedi the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] was able to destroy Toth's [[Hex Deployer]]s, then defeat the [[Sabaoth destroyer|Sabaoth Destroyers]] that Toth brought in as reinforcements. The [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Nym/Legends|Nym]] and allied [[Starship/Legends|ships]] arrived shortly thereafter in support of the squadron; with their help, Toth's plans were waylaid and Gallia was able to [[Death/Legends|kill]] him.<ref name="JS" />

Geonosis turned out to be the opening battle of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]-wide [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="NEC" /> and in the war Brand became a [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]].<ref name="HNN2">{{InsiderCite|65|Republic HoloNet News Core Edition 14:2:12}}</ref> Nine months after the Battle of Geonosis,<ref name="NEC" /> Brand and his clone [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] went to [[Bassadro]], a mineral-rich world that the Confederacy had occupied. The [[Battle of Bassadro]] lasted twelve [[Standard day/Legends|days]] and was ended when Brand led his [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] to the dormant [[Volcano/Legends|volcanic]] ranges that offered cover for the Separatist forces commanded by [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Domb Treetor]]. Brand ordered [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] strikes against the rock formations, removing the Separatists' cover and creating a storm of shrapnel that sliced through the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] village of [[Agao-Nir]], destroying many of the [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] stationed there,<ref name="HNN2" /> as well as four hundred unaffiliated miners.<ref name="NEC" /> Treetor surrendered, and Bassadro was retaken for the Republic.<ref name="HNN2" />

===Flight to Ganath===
{{Quote|They say a Jedi strong in the Force can rule those less powerful than himself. This I have done…I hope with justice!|Empatojayos Brand|<ref name="DEII 4" />}}
In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] declared the Jedi enemies of the Republic and [[Order 66/Legends|ordered]] their extermination, then [[Declaration of a New Order|transformed]] the Republic into a [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] with himself as [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. His new [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] set about [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|hunting down]] the Jedi,<ref name="NEC" /> and Brand was one of the last that Vader pursued.<ref name="Handbook">[[Star Wars Handbook 3: Dark Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 3: ''Dark Empire'']]</ref> He fled in a ship, but Vader followed,<ref name="DEII 4" /> and the two ships engaged in [[Starfighter combat/Legends|battle]].<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> Brand lost, and his vessel was destroyed near [[Ganathan Space]], an isolated pocket of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] within the [[Oktos Nebula/Legends|Oktos Nebula]]. Brand's body was ruined, and the Jedi fell through the [[Radiation/Legends|radioactive]] [[Gas/Legends|gas]] [[Cloud/Legends|cloud]] in a [[Environment suit/Legends|pressure suit]] until he was rescued by the [[Ganathan]]s, a race of people with limited space-faring capability who powered their technology with steam.<ref name="audio">[[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]]</ref> To keep him alive, Brand was outfitted with a [[Cybernetics/Legends|cyborg]] body made of Ganathan technology<ref name="DEII 4" /> but of his own design,<ref name="GSWU">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> his [[Limb/Legends|legs]] replaced with [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlifts]] and his arms with prostheses.<ref name="DEII 4" /> Seeing that the Ganathan people needed a strong ruler, Brand ascended to the position of [[King/Legends|king]], attempting to keep the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi creeds]] alive in his rule.<ref name="Handbook" />

Brand believed that Vader had killed all the remaining Jedi and gave up hope.<ref name="audio" /> With no contact of the outside world, he remained unaware of Vader's eventual [[Battle of Endor/Legends|death]], or of Palpatine's death and [[Transfer essence|return]] in a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body, and assumed that Vader had become a powerful [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] in charge of many [[Star system/Legends|star systems]].<ref name="DEII 4" />

===Return to the fight===
{{Quote|I will join you in this fight. The Jedi Knights must rise again.|Empatojayos Brand, to Leia Organa Solo|<ref name="DEII 4" />}}
[[File:Brand_meeting.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Brand meets the crew of the ''Millennium Falcon.'']]
In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> another ship penetrated the gas cloud: a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] vessel called the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], carrying [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], his copilot [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and Jedi Purge survivor [[Vima-Da-Boda]], all fleeing the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]. Upon entering the cloud, the damaged ''Falcon'' nearly ran into the Ganathan [[Battleship/Legends|battleship]] ''[[Robida Colossus]]'', which towed them back to Ganath. While they were en route, Ganathan Tower Command contacted Brand, presuming he would be interested to know of the outsiders. Brand met them at the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], and immediately recognizing Leia and Vima-Da-Boda as Jedi, ordered the guards surrounding them to lower their guns. Brand identified himself to them as Jedi and the two women confirmed it through the Force.<ref name="DEII 4" />

The ''Falcon'' was given repairs, and Brand oversaw the replacement of the vessel's [[Missile/Legends|missiles]] with a Ganathan [[Lightning gun (Ganath)|lightning gun]], which he believed to be a superior weapon. Joining the crew of the ''Falcon'' for a test run of the ship's new systems, Brand spoke with his fellow Jedi about the changes that had taken place in his absence from [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] events, including the present [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|fight]] between the New Republic and the reincarnated Palpatine. When he heard of Leia's brother [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], a Jedi who intended to rebuild a [[New Jedi Order]], Brand decided to contribute to the return of the Jedi Knights. He contacted his men, leaving the [[Ganathan space fleet]] in the hands of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Ussor]] and having Ussor relay news of his departure to the council. The ''Falcon'' left Ganathan Space and moved into the gas cloud, only to emerge right in front of Fett's [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']]. The ''Falcon''{{'s}} new lightning gun made quick work of ''Slave I'', shorting out its systems and sending it spiraling into the cloud as the ''Falcon'' made the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump to the safeworld of [[New Alderaan/Legends|New Alderaan]].<ref name="DEII 5">[[Dark Empire II 5|''Dark Empire II'' 5]]</ref>

On New Alderaan, Han acquainted Brand with starships in service to the New Republic, including the [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] and [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing starfighters]]. It was not long after their arrival that Brand sighted an unidentified ship entering New Alderaan's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]].<ref name="audio" /> For security reasons, it did not broadcast any comm signals, making it impossible to identify until it entered firing range. Brand rushed to warn Leia and her friend [[Winter Celchu|Winter]], but Leia soon [[Force sense/Legends|sensed]] that it was Skywalker returning. Skywalker, several Jedi recruits in tow, informed them that their [[Pinnacle Base|base]] at the moon [[Da Soocha V]] had been [[Destruction of Da Soocha V|destroyed]] by Palpatine's [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], the [[Galaxy Gun]]; not knowing that the [[New Republic Provisional Council|New Republic leaders]] had been evacuated beforehand, he believed them to be dead. Despite the perceived tragedy, Skywalker pushed to continue training the next generation of Jedi, entrusting that of [[Jem Ysanna|Jem]] and [[Rayf Ysanna]], two [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] youths he had found on the planet [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], to Brand. Brand showed them the arts of telekinesis that he had learned from Yoda by lifting large rocks and teaching them to do the same. Skywalker later showed all the Jedi information that he had retrieved from Ossus on the art of [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]].<ref name="DEII 6" />

[[File:Brand_and_posse.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Brand, Kam Solusar, and Rayf Ysanna face the Emperor's Dark Side Adepts.]]
The Empire, meanwhile, had learned the location of New Alderaan by [[Torture/Legends|interrogating]] one of their captives, New Republic Major [[Ntthan]]. A [[Dark Side Elite|strike force]] led by [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Executor]] [[Xecr Nist]] was [[Battle of New Alderaan (Operation Shadow Hand)|dispatched]] to the world, its goal to retrieve the Force-sensitive [[Solo twins (disambiguation)|children]] of the Solos, [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina]] and [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]]. As Nist and a number of other [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]] converged on the building where the Solo children slept, Brand, Rayf Ysanna, and Jedi Knight [[Kam Solusar]] intercepted them and engaged in a [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|duel]] of [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] and [[Force power/Legends|Force prowess]]. As Brand fought off multiple enemies at the same [[Time/Legends|time]], Solusar dueled Nist, and the attack was successfully repelled, though the [[darksider]]s' secondary mission against Skywalker left Jem Ysanna dead. The victory was short-lived, though, as a number of [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]] soon joined the fight, laying waste to the New Republic settlement. The New Alderaan populace was evacuated, Brand and a number of others escaping in the [[Freighter/Legends|cargo vessel]] ''[[Starhook Ten]]''. Their destination was [[Nespis VIII]], an abandoned [[City/Legends|city]] in space. There, the New Alderaan evacuees rendezvoused with the escaped Republic leadership. Skywalker, Brand, Ysanna, and Solusar convened to discuss their plight, and Brand encouraged the others not to give into despair. They were interrupted by the announcement of the [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] of Leia's third child, [[Anakin Solo]].<ref name="DEII 6" />

===The end of Palpatine===
{{Quote|Luke…Palpatine will die with me. He will never return. The Force—and all the Jedi who went before us… will make sure of that. Goodbye, my friend.|Empatojayos Brand's last words|<ref name="EE 2" />}}
Brand later oversaw the removal of the lightning gun from the ''Falcon'', as Chewbacca wanted his missiles back. Unfortunately for the Republic, not long after, their location was betrayed to the Empire by an Imperial [[Spy/Legends|spy]], who was posing as a [[Technician/Legends|technician]] named [[Jenks (Imperial)|Jenks]]. When Brand and Han confronted Jenks, he fired his [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] upon them, hitting Brand, but the Jedi was saved by his suit's backup system.<ref name="EEaudio">[[Empire's End (audio drama)|''Empire's End'' audio drama]]</ref> Before an evacuation could be issued, a [[particle disintegrator warhead]] came out of hyperspace near Nespis VIII, fired by the Galaxy Gun. Fortunately for them, it was a dud, and the evacuation was pulled off before a second missile arrived and [[Destruction of the Nespis VIII spaceport|destroyed]] the city. Once in deep space, the Jedi and Republic leaders gathered to discuss their next phase of operations. Skywalker decided that he, Brand, Solusar, and Ysanna would go to Ossus to scout it as a possible location for a base, while the Solos and Vima-Da-Boda would find a safe hiding spot. The Jedi arrived in orbit of Ossus to find [[Imperial hunter (starship)|Imperial ships]] leaving; landing, the Jedi learned that the Empire had kidnapped members of the Ysanna tribe, and Solusar's [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] readings pointed them to [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]], a world where [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader had once owned a personal [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]] called [[Bast Castle]].<ref name="EE 1">[[Empire's End 1|''Empire's End'' 1]]</ref>

[[File:Brand_Vjun.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Brand fights in Bast Castle.]]
[[Mission to Vjun (Operation Shadow Hand)|Arriving on Vjun]], the Jedi sent Solusar's [[I-7 Howlrunner]] starship ahead on [[Autopilot/Legends|automated systems]] to crash into the wall of the castle, opening the way for the Jedi. Disembarking from Skywalker's ship, the ''[[Jedi Explorer II]]'', the Jedi did battle with Nist and the dark side warriors who swarmed to meet them. Skywalker eventually ended the fight by using the Force to topple a gigantic statue of Vader onto the darksiders. They found the captured Ysanna frozen in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]; Nist, the only survivor among the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]], told them that only Palpatine knew how to free them, and Skywalker was determined to find the Emperor and end the fight against him for good.<ref name="EE 1" />

In [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> Palpatine was on his way to the [[Japrael system/Legends|Onderon system]] in pursuit of the Solos. With his clone bodies deteriorating, the Emperor needed a new vessel to contain his [[Soul/Legends|soul]] and decided on Anakin Solo. Though the Solos escaped an ambush by Palpatine's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Eclipse II]]'', near [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], they had to land the ''Falcon'' on the surface of the planet, while the ''Eclipse II'' took [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the world. Chewbacca and Vima-Da-Boda later left Onderon in the ''Falcon'', hoping to serve as a decoy while the Solos took refuge in the Onderonian wilderness. Brand and the other Jedi returned to the [[New Republic Defense Fleet|Republic fleet]] transport [[Vindicator (New Republic)|''Vindicator'']], where they met Chewbacca and Vima-Da-Boda and learned of Palpatine's whereabouts. A fleet was assembled to hit the ''Eclipse II'' and bring down the Emperor.<ref name="EE 2" />

The fleet [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|arrived]] at Onderon and engaged the Emperor's flagship in battle. When Solusar sensed that Palpatine was not aboard the vessel, Skywalker, Brand, Vima-Da-Boda, and Ysanna descended to the planet's surface in the ''Jedi Explorer II'', certain that he would have gone there to find the Solos. They landed at [[Iziz Starport]] and soon located Palpatine's [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle|shuttle]]. The Jedi were shortly joined by Han Solo, who had returned from the [[Kira Fortress]] in the wilderness where his wife and children were in hiding to meet Skywalker. The party assaulted the shuttle, fighting [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and members of the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]], and entered it to find not Palpatine, but one of his darksiders serving as a decoy. Solo realized that Palpatine had gone to the fortress, and they set off in pursuit in the ''Jedi Explorer II''.<ref name="EE 2" />

Brand and his companions were not fast enough, however; Palpatine had reached the fortress before they did and attacked Leia, attempting to take her child Anakin. In the midst of the struggle, Brand charged in, informing Skywalker, who was following him, that Palpatine was there. Palpatine hit Brand with a blast of [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], grievously injuring Brand and knocking him to the ground. Rayf Ysanna struck down one of Palpatine's darksider companions, [[T'iaz]], but was in turn killed by another blast of lightning from the Emperor. However, the second attack weakened Palpatine, a fact that Brand made Skywalker aware of. Skywalker gave Palpatine a chance to surrender, but the Emperor instead charged for Anakin Solo, attempting to take the child's body to hold his own soul. Before he could reach Anakin, Han Solo shot the Emperor with his [[DL-44 heavy blaster pistol/Legends|blaster]].<ref name="EE 2" />

[[File:Brand_Dripping.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Brand, with the spirit of Palpatine inside him, hands Anakin Solo to his mother.]]
In his last burst of life, Palpatine's spirit separated from his body and moved towards the child. Brand regained control of his repulsors and launched himself across at Anakin, catching the child and protecting him with the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]]. Brand grasped Palpatine's spirit and took it [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] within himself. Being eaten alive by the darkness within him, Brand sensed the despair and anger within Palpatine as he died. Palpatine spoke through the Jedi one last time before he died, cursing the [[Skywalker family/Legends|Skywalker bloodline]]. Brand bade farewell and died, carrying Palpatine's spirit with him to be held [[Prison/Legends|prisoner]] for eternity by every Jedi who had become one with the Force, never to be resurrected again.<ref name="EE 2" />

The next day, Skywalker recorded a message for Anakin Solo telling him of the events of Palpatine's resurrection and defeat. He closed by bidding Anakin to remember Brand and the Ysannas for the sacrifices they had made to ensure that he grow up and live a long life.<ref name="EGTTF">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> Later, Jedi Knight [[Tam Azur-Jamin]] chronicled Brand in his essay ''[[Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena]]''.<ref name="DTF">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/81/indexp7.html|text=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena|int=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena (article)}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I am grateful that Brand was with us. He was a greater Jedi than I.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="EGTTF" />}}
[[File:Empatojayos_Brand_Ganathan-HBDE.jpg|thumb|Empatojayos Brand, king of the Ganathans]]
In his youth, Brand was a vain and confident individual.<ref name="NEGTC" /> Brand ascended to his kingship out of a desire to see strong leadership amongst the Ganathans.<ref name="Handbook" /> Having heard that those strong in the Force could rule those less powerful, Brand attempted to do just that, hoping that he could do so justly.<ref name="DEII 4" /> Despite the Jedi Order being destroyed, Brand made sure to bring justice in accordance with its tenets.<ref name="Handbook" /> In his essay, Azur-Jamin proclaimed Brand to be a more just monarch than any other, using him as proof that cybernetics did not reduce one's Humanity.<ref name="DTF" /> Brand's own subjects considered him to be a man of honor, particularly because he was a Jedi.<ref name="DEII 5" />

When the opportunity came to rebuild the Jedi, Brand was eager to do so.<ref name="Handbook" /> In the wake of the attacks on the Jedi and New Republic, and the deaths of many of his companions, Brand remained optimistic, counseling his fellow Jedi not to give into despair and stressing that that would be the path to the dark side.<ref name="DEII 6" /> Brand fought for the sake of the Jedi and the Solo children several times, ultimately sacrificing his life to preserve Anakin Solo's.<ref name="Handbook" /> Luke Skywalker considered him to be a hero and a greater Jedi than himself.<ref name="EGTTF" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|Rayf…Jem…''Master Yoda'' taught me this skill, many years ago. Are you ready to finish lifting that ''heavy stone'' without me?|Brand instructing Rayf and Jem Ysanna in the use of telekinesis|<ref name="DEII 6" />}}
As an older man, the Force was strong in Brand. As a Jedi Knight, he was skilled in the use of telekinesis and was trained in the use of a lightsaber.<ref name="DEII 6" /> He had the ability to [[Wall of light|capture and contain]] the spirit of Palpatine, a skill unknown to Luke Skywalker at the time,<ref name="EGTTF" /> and could [[Mechu-deru|mentally control his robot body parts]] and use the light side to [[Force Light|shield]] a child from the Emperor's soul.<ref name="EE 2" /> He had a knowledge of technology, both in ships and cybernetics, and took pride in the abilities of Ganathan technology.<ref name="DEII 5" /> Brand could also fly the Delta-7 ''Aethersprite''-class light interceptor.<ref name="JS" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|This 'droid suit' is a sophisticated survival system. It can keep me alive on the hottest—or coldest—planet in the galaxy. I could even survive for a year in the vacuum of space!|Empatojayos Brand|<ref name="DEII 4" />}}
[[File:Brand_Force.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Brand uses the Force and his built-in lightsaber.]]
Brand, after reconstruction, was over half machine.<ref name="DTF" /> His suit, made by combining Ganathan technology with his own modern technology,<ref name="Handbook" /> and of his own design,<ref name="GSWU" /> served as a replacement for his damaged body. Spherical, it lacked legs and was supported by repulsorlifts,<ref name="Handbook" /> which he controlled mentally.<ref name="EE 2" /> Brand could not sit while in it, but equipped with [[magnetic clamp]]s, the suit could be anchored to a bulkhead when a ship was underway.<ref name="audio" /> The suit could keep Brand alive on the hottest and coldest planets, even allowing him to survive for up to a year in the [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] of space<ref name="DEII 5" /> without eating.<ref name="audio" /> The suit was equipped with backup systems, allowing Brand to recover from [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] shots.<ref name="EEaudio" /> However, it could be temporarily disabled with Force lightning.<ref name="EE 2" /> Both of Brand's regular arms were prosthetic, and one had a lightsaber built into it,<ref name="DEII 6" /> but he could bring out two other arms when necessary. He had sensors built into the suit as well, which could detect lifeforms and even their gender. Brand's suit could also interface with a ship's [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting computer]].<ref name="EEaudio" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Brand_WTF.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Brand look-alike in ''Union 2''.]]
Empatojayos Brand was created for the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, by [[Tom Veitch]] and [[Cam Kennedy]], debuting in the [[Dark Empire II 4|fourth issue]],<ref name="DEII 4" /> which was published on [[March 21]], [[1995]].<ref name="Marvel DE 4">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54066/star_wars_dark_empire_ii_1994_4|text=Star Wars: Dark Empire II (1994) #4}}</ref> The character was featured in the sequel series ''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]'' and was killed in the [[Empire's End 2|second issue]] of that series,<ref name="EE 2" /> published on [[November 28]] of the same year.<ref name="Marvel EE 2">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54212/star_wars_empires_end_1995_2|text=Star Wars: Empire's End (1995) #2}}</ref> Brand was also featured in the audio drama adaptations of [[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'']] and [[Empire's End (audio drama)|''Empire's End'']], in which he was voiced by [[Joe Hacker]].<ref name="DE Collectors Edition">''[[Dark Empire: The Collector's Edition]]''</ref>

On [[December 8]] of [[1999]],<ref name="Marvel Union">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54635/star_wars_union_1999_2|text=Star Wars: Union (1999) #2}}</ref> a character greatly resembling Brand also appeared in the background of the [[Wedding of Luke Skywalker and Mara Jade|marriage]] scene in the [[Union 2|second issue]] of the comic series ''[[Star Wars: Union]]'',<ref name="Union">[[Union 2|''Union'' 2]]</ref> set [[19 ABY/Legends|eight years after]] the character's death.<ref name="NEC" /> In the [[2002]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]'', Brand's name is assigned to one of the eponymous Jedi starfighters in the game's final mission, but the character is not otherwise referred to, and he is given no lines.<ref name="JS" />
